title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary: The reviewer suggests two changes: (1) extract the shared RBAC rules from `Role` and `ClusterRole` templates into a named Helm partial/helper to prevent drift when rules change, and (2) add a `Namespace` field to `ManagerConfig` so namespace-scoped operation can be configured programmatically, not only via the `--namespace` CLI flag.
external_feedback:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The `Role` in `templates/role.yaml` deliberately duplicates the `ClusterRole` rules; consider extracting the shared rules into a named template/partial and including it from both files to avoid future drift when RBAC rules change.
- The new `--namespace` CLI flag is only configured via flag parsing in `Run`; if `ManagerConfig` is used to construct managers programmatically, consider adding a `Namespace` field there so callers can opt into namespace scoping without going through global flags.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The `Role` in `templates/role.yaml` deliberately duplicates the `ClusterRole` rules; consider extracting the shared rules into a named template/partial and including it from both files to avoid future drift when RBAC rules change.
- The new `--namespace` CLI flag is only configured via flag parsing in `Run`; if `ManagerConfig` is used to construct managers programmatically, consider adding a `Namespace` field there so callers can opt into namespace scoping without going through global flags.
~~~

</details>
